rulings.Case Title: RONALD C. BROOKS VS. WILL LIGHTBOURNE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Pro. 1094.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 23, 2015, Line 1, PETITIONER RONALD BROOKS' Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Pro. 1094.5. Writ granted. The Department of Social Services is ordered to reverse its decision of July 17, 2013 and remove the MFG designation from petitioner's son for all time periods, past and present, while he is in the custody of petitioner and provide benefits withheld from petitioner because of the MFG designation of his son. While the notice provisions of W & I code section 11450.04 are ambiguous and can fairly be construed to support the positions advocated by both sides, the deference accorded to an agency's interpretation of statutory language, the context of the statutory language, and furtherance of the purposes of section 11450.04 all indicate that the interpretation advocated by respondents -- that notice given to Avila suffices as notice to petitioner -- is the more reasonable one. This is particularly true since, per petitioner's interpretation, had he received proper notice, his son could permissibly be designated an MFG as to petitioner's AU, yet there is no reasonable way for notice to have been provided to petitioner other than by giving notice to Avila. The hearsay rule in the Administrative Procedure Act (Govt. Code section 11513(d)) relied on by petitioner does not apply to the hearing held in this case due to W & I code sections 10953 and 10955 (see also Govt. Code section 11501). However, per W & I code section 10955 and MPP section 22-050, the hearing was governed by the requirements that "all testimony shall be submitted under oath or affirmation" and "evidence shall be admitted if it is the sort of evidence on which responsible persons are accustomed to rely in the conduct of serious affairs." Applying the independent judgment standard of review, there was insufficient evidence to support the finding of the ALJ -- adopted by and necessary to the Department's decision denying petitioner's request for benefits for his son -- that the required notice was given to Avila about the MFG rules. This is because the only even conceivably probative evidence about notice to Avila of the MFG rules were the unsworn statements of Mr. Gomez that Avila's file showed that such notice was given. Mr. Gomez stated that he reviewed Avila's file, yet he chose not to introduce any portion of that file into evidence and refused to disclose it to petitioner. (See MPP section 22-049 (petitioner was entitled to "Examine all documents prior to and during the hearing.").) Regardless of the merits of Mr. Gomez's assertion of confidentiality of the Avila file, the absence of any evidence from that file discloses that the "evidence" that was provided by Mr. Gomez fell below "the sort of evidence on which responsible persons are accustomed to rely in the conduct of serious affairs." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO LA COUNTY ** SANTA BARBARA CHANNELKEEPER VS. STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant And Respondent City Of San Buenaventura'S Notice Of Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 23, 2015, Line 2, RESPONDENT CITY OF BUENAVENTURA'S Motion To Strike. The City of San Buenaventura's demurrer to and motion to strike the first count is overruled and denied with twenty days to answer. The City has not shown that there is a misjoinder or that any other party is necessary or indispensable to the adjudication of the first count. The complaint does not clearly disclose that it is time-barred. The applicable limitations statute is Code of Civil Procedure section 338, not Water Code section 13330. The notice requirements of the federal Clean Water Act and the federal Endangered Species Act are inapplicable to this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO LA COUNTY ** SANTA BARBARA CHANNELKEEPER VS. STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 23, 2015, Line 2, RESPONDENT STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. The State Water Resources Control Board's demurrer to the second, third, fourth and fifth counts is overruled as to all counts with twenty days leave to answer. The second, third and fourth counts adequately allege that the Board failed to perform mandatory duties. (See Elmore v. Imperial Irrigation District (1984) 159 Cal. App. 3d 185). The fifth count adequately alleges that the Board abused its discretion. (See Ellena v. Department of Insurance (2014) 230 Cal. App. 4th 198, 211). rulings.Case Title: PING CHING WONG et al VS. TSUNG TSIN ASSOCIATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 23, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANTS EUGENE CHAN AND BIANNE CHAN'S Motion For Attorney Fees. Denied. Moving parties failed to meet their burden of setting forth specific facts supporting the amount of the cost of proof sanctions they seek. (Weil and Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2014) 8:1413.1 and 8:1413.1a)). Paragraph 9 of Mr. Chen's declaration makes the conclusory statement, without any time records, that he spent "160 hours ¿ in dealing with this litigation, including ¿ trial." Not only was no trial held in this case, but Mr. Chen made no effort to show the amount of time he spent "proving" the matters denied by plaintiffs as opposed to time spent on the case generally. Moreover, a review of the successful motion for summary adjudication, which is the predicate for this motion, shows that the motion was very simple, relying on only seven purported undisputed facts and involving minimal legal research. rulings.Case Title: MAUREEN KEATING VS. DANIEL BERKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 23, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions. Pro Tem JudgeVedica Puri, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is DENIED because the discovery at issue is unduly overburdensome. The Court was unable to find defendant's declaration supporting the need for additional discovery in the courtesy copies provided. Regardless, discovery including upwards of 75 RFAs combined with the requisite17.1 form interrogatory is not proportionate to issues in this case. Specifically, the manner in which the RFAs are drafted, calling out apparently each line of the Complaint, appear intended to harass instead of narrow the issues to be tried in the case, which is the purpose of RFAs. The request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to vpuri@pillsburycoleman.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CIARAN LONG VS. 37DEGREE FILTERS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 23, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANTS HUBB FILTERS, INC., CHRISTOPHER HUBBARD, JEFF MORGEN, AND GARY NELSON'S Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration. Granted on the condition that neither JAMS nor the arbitrator require plaintiff to pay any expenses beyond what he would be required to pay had this motion not be granted. The majority of the claims asserted by Mr. Long are covered by the parties' arbitration agreement. Because the promissory note claim falls outside the parties' arbitration agreement, that claim is stayed pending the results of the arbitration. By referencing the JAMS employment arbitration rules and procedures in their agreement, which in turn unmistakably disclose that arbitrability and validity of the arbitration clause are to be decided by the arbitrator, the parties delegated the issue of enforceability of the arbitration agreement to the arbitrator. (Universal Protection Service, L.P. v. Superior Court (2015) 234 Cal. App. 4th 1128). The Armendariz requirement that "when an employer imposes mandatory arbitration ¿ the arbitration ¿ process cannot generally require the employee to bear any type of expense that the employee would not be required to bear if he or she were free to bring the action in court" is applicable to plaintiff's claims and remains good law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
